text: CROP: Certifying Robust Policies for Reinforcement Learning through Functional Smoothing
Fan Wu1,Linyi Li1,Zijian Huang1,Yevgeniy Vorobeychik², Ding Zhao³,Bo Li1
1Universityof Ilinois at Urbana-Champaign ²Washington Universityin St.Louis3Carnegie Mellon University

list_items: Testing and improving the robustness of safety-critical applications before their massive deployment to real-world is of great importance.
Robustness certification for a given learning algorithm is needed to end the repeated game between theattackersand defenders.

text: Testing-time evasion attack on agent's observation

Adversary can apply $\ell _ { 2 }$ -bounded perturbations $B ^ { \varepsilon } = \{ \delta \in \mathbb { R } ^ { n } \| \| \delta \| _ { 2 } \leq \varepsilon \}$ to input state observations of the agent

text: Provide test-time certification for the performance of the trained agent in the perturbed environment

list_items: 1.Providing criteria for robustness certification
2.Proposing certification strategies corresponding to different certification criteria

text: i.e., the policy produces the same action on state s with or without perturbation.
Lowerboundofperturbed cumulative reward $\_$ : with perturbations at every step bounded by $B ^ { \varepsilon }$ ,we have $\underline { { J } } \leq J _ { \varepsilon } ( \pi )$

list_items: 1.Trajectory exploration and expansion: obtain the lower bound $\underline { { J } } _ { \varepsilon }$ forε (DFS)
2.Perturbation magnitude growth: obtain a new $\varepsilon ^ { \prime }$ (priority queue)
·Outcome of the algorithm: $\left\{ \left( \varepsilon _ { i } , \underline { { J } } _ { \varepsilon _ { i } } \right) \right\} _ { i = 1 } ^ { | C | }$
·Four RLenvironments: Freeway,Pong,CartPole, Highway
Nine DQN variants:StdTrain, GaussAug,AdvTrain,SA-MDP (PGD,CVX), RadialRL,CARRL,NoisyNet, GradDQN
·Two certification criteria: per state-action, cumulative reward
，Three certification algorithms:CROP-LoAct,CROP-GRe,CROP-LoRe

image_footnote: Fig.Robustness certification for per-stateactionin terms ofcertifiedradiusratalltime steps.

list_items: GradDQN and RadialRL achieve the highest certified radius on Freeway.
Periodic patterns can be observed in Pong.
Certified results largely match empirical observations.

img_path: output/CROP: Certifying Robust Policies for Reinforcement Learning through Functional Smoothing_poster/visuals/images/fig_5.jpg

list_items: ·Under global smoothing,percentile bound is tighter than expectation bound.

For CROP-LoRe,different RLalgorithms demonstrate different behavior in different regions of attack magnitudeε---GradDQN achieves higher lower bound under smaller ε,while RadialRL is the dominant one under larger ε.
·The certification is relatively tight, compared with the empirical result.
